question:
Pump.fun >$4B market cap (FDV) one day after launch?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Pump.fun's token is greater than $4,000,000,000 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not be considered a launch.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 24 hours after launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Pump.fun doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2025, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No."

evidences.title: Pump Fun: The launch of PUMP, the ultimate extract or a new ...
evidences.content: ### ICO Process The public phase of the ICO took place directly on the Pump Fun interface, as well as via six CEX partners (Bybit, Bitget, Kraken, Gate, KuCoin, and MEXC). The introductory price was set at $0.004 per PUMP token, for a fully diluted valuation (FDV) of $4 billion, with terms equivalent to those for institutional investors (tokens unlocked at TGE). According to figures provided by Pump Fun, the 125 billion PUMP tokens on offer (about $500 million) sold out in 12 minutes. Despite the scale of the funds raised, this lightning sale demonstrates the extremely high demand for the token during the ICO. The result surprised many observers, especially given surveys showing that most investors didn’t intend to participate. [...] Current revenue: Pump Fun generates around $500 million per year, with a solid track record even in quieter market periods. Available cash: The protocol reportedly holds close to $1 billion in treasury, a powerful lever for innovation or potential strategic acquisitions. Distribution: Up to 75% of the token supply could be used for community incentives or airdrops, mechanically limiting short-term sell pressure. Valuation/revenue ratio: At a $4-6 bi...

evidences.content: additional fees once a token reaches liquidity thresholds. A handful of tokens gain traction, but the “house” earns on them all. Every trade is monetized: token creators and social contributors receive a share of activity. Imagine the NYSE splitting commissions with CNBC, it sounds absurd, but Pump.fun has made it reality. Of course, the risks are enormous: regulatory scrutiny, reputational fallout, lawsuits by failed issuers, and the possibility of systemic collapse. Yet in the meantime, the platform appears to generate millions of dollars in revenue, daily! By the Numbers - In July 2025, Pump.fun raised $500 million in 12 minutes, pricing PUMP at $0.004 and implying a fully diluted valuation (FDV) of about $4 billion. - The PUMP token’s FDV currently fluctuates in the $4–5.6 billion [...] PUMP token’s FDV currently fluctuates in the $4–5.6 billion range. - Creators receive distributions of $10 million, weekly. - The platform has spent over $60 million on PUMP buybacks. The Token Issuance The platform raised $400 million in under 10 minutes, pricing the coin ~30% below perpetual futures markets, you wonder who was buying those perpetuals! For early coin buyers who hedged via pe...

evidences.title: pump.fun - Wikipedia
evidences.content: pump.fun (commonly referred to as Pump) is a cryptocurrency launchpad for the Solana "Solana (blockchain platform)") blockchain that enables users to create tokens and trade them immediately on the platform, as well as to launch them onto decentralized exchanges, a process known as "graduation". The platform was launched on January 19, 2024, by Noah Tweedale, Alon Cohen and Dylan Kerler. evidences.title: Understanding Pump.fun: Solana's Token Creation Platform
evidences.content: ### Pump.fun Platform Overview Established by co-founders Alon Cohen, Dylan Kerler, and Noah Tweedale in January 2024, Pump.fun provides a protocol enabling token creation within minutes without fees. The platform has generated over 11.9 million tokens, accounting for up to 71% of Solana's daily token launches. Among Pump.fun's most debated features stands its integrated livestreaming capability. This addition followed meme coin developers streaming on Kick and Telegram while executing humorous, bizarre, and hazardous stunts to inflate their tokens. The native functionality triggered polarizing streams, leading to temporary suspension due to safety concerns. [...] From 1 trillion total tokens, 33% were distributed via initial offering, 13% allocated to existing investors, and 20% reserved for Pump.fun's team—now exceeding 70 employees. The token announcement confirmed an upcoming airdrop. Tokenomics designate 24% for "Community and Ecosystem Initiatives" and 3% for "Livestreaming," fueling speculation about substantial airdrops for traders, deployers, and streamers. No confirmed airdrop date exists currently. Following token launch, Pump.fun initiated buybacks exceeding $19 mill...

evidences.title: Pump.fun PUMP Token Launch: Key Dates and Full Details | CCN.com
evidences.content: Logo News 5 min read # Pump.fun PUMP Token Launch: Key Dates and Full Details Last Updated 10 July 2025 Giuseppe Ciccomascolo Authors By Giuseppe Ciccomascolo Edited by Insha Zia Pump.fun might finally be getting its own token—or maybe not just yet. Pump.fun might finally be getting its own token—or maybe not just yet. | Credit: Joel Kowsky/NASA via Getty Images Share Key Takeaways Pump.fun lets anyone launch and trade memecoins on Solana—no coding needed. Its first official token sale was reportedly set for July 12–15, 2025. Gate.io briefly posted the launch page and then deleted it, sparking confusion among investors. However, Pump.fun then confirmed the token sale window. Pump.fun might finally be getting its own token, or maybe not just yet. [...] ## What is Pump.fun? Launched in January 2024 by a pseudonymous creator, Alon, Pump.fun is a Solana-based no-code platform where anyone can launch and trade memecoins in minutes. It uses a bonding curve model. Prices go up as more tokens are bought, keeping things transparent and fair. Once a token reaches a $69,000 market cap, it is listed on Raydium, and part of the supply is burned to support price growth. There are no pre-sales...

evidences.title: Pump Fun Token Listing
evidences.content: ‍ ## History and Evolution of Pump Fun Pump Fun launched in January 2024 amid Solana's memecoin surge. Creators saw a gap in easy token deployment. Early days focused on simple tools for fun coins. Adoption grew fast as Solana boomed. By spring, daily launches hit thousands. Growth tied to Solana's low-fee environment. Pump Fun Solana synergy fueled this rise. Milestones included crossing $700 million in revenue from fees. Users created over a million tokens. Celebrities and influencers jumped in. However, challenges emerged. Market saturation slowed activity recently. Daily volume dipped below $100 million. Competition heated up. LetsBonk (Bonk.fun) grabbed market share with similar features. It offers better rewards for holders. Pump Fun adapted by planning expansions. [...] Excitement builds ahead of the July 12, 2025, launch. Investors scramble for details on this Pump Fun crypto opportunity. As a key player in the Pump Fun Solana ecosystem, the platform has already generated huge revenue. evidences.title: From release rules to sales usage, the details of the pump.fun token sale are out
evidences.content: Sales terms and pricing Private sales and public sales are priced uniformly at $0.004 per token, and all ICO tokens will be fully unlocked on the first day of listing. Regardless of what the official website shows, tokens will be distributed within 48-72 hours after the sale ends, and can be freely transferred within 48-72 hours after distribution. Payment methods support USDC or USDT. Sales timing and participation restrictions Sale period: July 12, 22:00 to July 15, 22:00 (lasting 3 days). Participation requirements: KYC must be completed, users from the UK, USA, and certain restricted jurisdictions are prohibited from participating; EU users must confirm voluntary purchase. [...] Image 4 How are tokens released? According to the PUMP release schedule, tokens allocated to the team (20%) and existing investors (13%) will gradually unlock starting from July 2026; the portion allocated to community and ecosystem programs (24%) will gradually unlock from the first day of token issuance and will be fully allocated by July 2026; other tokens allocated for ICO, liquidity + exchanges, and the foundation will be fully unlocked on the first day of token issuance.

evidences.content: ## Pump.fun (PUMP) Project Overview Since its January 2024 debut by founders Noah Tweedale, Alon Cohen, and Dylan Kerler, Pump.fun has facilitated over 11 million token launches and generated more than $700 million in protocol fees, rapidly establishing itself as the go-to hub for meme-coin enthusiasts on Solana (SOL) by democratizing token creation and trading. Its social features: live streaming for token pitches, real-time leaderboards, and integrated chat have cultivated a vibrant community that thrives on rapid launches, spirited debates, and the thrill of chasing potential “100x” tokens. evidences.title: Meme Coin Mania on Pump.fun: An Economic and Legal Analysis
evidences.content: revenue from its 1% trading fee. On peak activity days, Pump.fun has been responsible for the lion’s share of Solana’s on-chain activity; according to Dune Analytics data reported by media outlets, Pump.fun accounted for approximately 71 % of new token launches on Solana on peak weekend days (e.g., 71.1 % Saturday, 69.5 % Sunday). Pump.fun’s rapid ascent culminated in a July 2025 token offering of its native token ($PUMP) that raised $600 million in 12 minutes, driving the platform’s valuation into the billions. [...] With that in mind, token velocity – the rate at which coins are traded and then abandoned for the next trend – is extremely high. In practice, meme coins on Pump.fun often have lifespans of mere hours or days of intense trading before interest shifts. For instance, one hyped coin launched by an internet personality in December 2024 lost 95% of its value within hours of launch, causing an uproar among late investors. Yet, on that same day, traders had already moved on to pouring funds into a different meme coin (a token themed after a famous pet squirrel) which surged to a $1 billion valuation in short order. This illustrates how quickly capital rotates between flee...

evidences.title: Over 47K Meme Coins Launch on Pump.fun in 24 Hours, Meme Index Lets Investors Tap Into Them, ICO Tops $3.3M - Finance PR
evidences.content: Meme coin creation has accelerated due to Solana’s Pump.fun, which launched about a year ago. Since September 2024, new tokens springing from the launchpad have been on an upward trajectory, coinciding with two of the best months for crypto gains in October and November. The trend hasn’t stopped, as in the past 24 hours, about 47,053 new meme coins launched on Solana’s Pump.fun, according to data from Dune Analytics. Despite the seemingly herculean task of hitting a home run with these millions of tokens, some end up making a good profit. For instance, a token called Rope ($ROPE) saw its market cap surge to nearly $6 million, with about 15 traders realizing over $10,000 gains each, per DEX screener. evidences.title: Predicting the success of new crypto-tokens: the Pump.fun case - arXiv
evidences.content: We study the dynamics of token launched on Pump.fun, a Solana-based launchpad platform, to identify the determinants of the token success. Pump.fun employs a bonding curve mechanism to bootstrap initial liquidity possibly leading to graduation to the on-chain market, which can be seen as a token success. We build predictive models of the probability of graduation conditional on the current amount of Solana locked in the bonding curve and a set of explanatory variables that capture structural and behavioral aspects of the launch process. Conditioning the graduation probability on these variables significantly improves its predictive power, providing insights into early-stage market behavior, speculative and manipulative dynamics, and the informational efficiency of bonding-curve-based [...] Beyond contract-level homogeneity, Pump.fun also embeds an endogenous and protocol-level notion of early success. Each token begins trading on a virtual constant-product bonding curve that governs price formation and liquidity accumulation. Graduation occurs deterministically once cumulative buy pressure pushes the bonding curve beyond a fixed threshold of bonded Solana, triggering migration t...

Fund's second challenge, which is the platform's economics. While Pump Fund's bonding curve and fair launch design aim to democratize token creation, the odds for most users are stacked against them. Only a fraction of tokens ever graduate to a major deck. Most fail outright or disappear as soon as the hype dies. Analysis by Solidus Labs showed that over 98% of pump fund tokens were either abandoned or part of pump and dump schemes and just a tiny percentage [...] also puts Pumpot Fund in direct competition with platforms like Radium's Launch Lab and other new projects offering their own fee sharing and token launch features. evidences.title: pump.fun - Wikipedia
evidences.content: ## History [edit] The domain name was first registered on September 19, 2023, with the platform launching four months later on January 19, 2024. The website was started by an anonymous developer known as Alon, tweeting that he wanted to make it "the most fun place on the internet". It was later revealed from public documents associated with the website that the owners were a trio of English entrepreneurs named Noah Tweedale, Alon Cohen and Dylan Kerler, who say they started the site due to their frustrations with trading memecoins and the risks of getting rug pulled. Pump.fun was estimated to have generated $60 million in transaction fees (a 1% cut of trades on the platform) in the first half of 2024, with over a million tokens having been created by July 2024. [...] Since its launch, pump has generated nearly $800 million in revenue. Its ICO in July 2025 raised $600 million in 12 minutes through a public sale as well as an additional $720 million in private sales, giving the company about $1.3 billion in cash. $465.5 million of this was cashed out by pump into USDC in November 2025 as its token, PUMP, fell more than 25%. The platform has been compared with social media platform...

evidences.content: The upstart has dominated other metrics as well. In the past 24 hours, LetsBonk accounted for $163 million in volume, about 800% more than Pump.fun. In that span, Pump.fun had just 34 graduates. On token launchpads like Pump.fun and LetsBonk, a newly launched memecoin graduates when it raises enough Solana on its bonding curve to be listed on a decentralised exchange. ## Recent actions Pump.fun's recent actions have also led to criticism. evidences.title: Meme Coin Mania on Pump.fun: An Economic and Legal Analysis | STORM Partners | STORM Partners
evidences.content: Pump.fun fundamentally lowered barriers to launching a token: with a Solana wallet and a few clicks, users can deploy a new coin and have it immediately tradeable via an automated bonding curve market maker. Every coin is a “fair launch” with no initial liquidity required, which democratized participation but also unleashed an unprecedented flood of meme coins. By January 2025 – only one year after launch – over 6 million unique tokens had been created on Pump.fun. By mid-2025, the scale of activity had exploded. Pump.fun had reportedly spawned more than 11 million tokens, at times responsible for roughly 70 percent of all new token launches on Solana — a level of dominance rarely seen on any blockchain. Most of these coins are purely speculative “meme coins” with no function beyond [...] With that in mind, token velocity – the rate at which coins are traded and then abandoned for the next trend – is extremely high. In practice, meme coins on Pump.fun often have lifespans of mere hours or days of intense trading before interest shifts. For instance, one hyped coin launched by an internet personality in December 2024 lost 95% of its value within hours of launch, causing an uproar...

Particularly noteworthy—judging by the media meltdown it caused—was the launch of the meme coins TRUMP and MELANIA ahead of Donald Trump's presidential inauguration. evidences.title: Mayhem Mode | Pump
evidences.content: ## The Mayhem Mode Vision Pump.fun is the market leader in coin launches. However, not every coin receives the attention it deserves, and oftentimes good coins are throttled by poor price action or loss of attention at key levels. Mayhem Mode is a completely unique, first-of-its-kind autonomous AI trading agent which aims to give new coins the support they need to reach new heights. Mayhem Mode aims to serve as a replacement for market makers or similar tools which typically require projects to give away supply of their token, pay for services or do unnecessary research into potentially-shady operations. Users should be able to rely on transparent, proven, and trusted tools with no requirement for prior knowledge of market dynamics or crypto know-how. [...] When Mayhem Mode is enabled, the Agent will mint 1,000,000,000 additional tokens (2,000,000,000 total token supply) which will be used to trade the coin by buying and selling the coin with equal probabilities in a random walk within the first 24 hours of the coin's creation.
